items.title: Comey Indictment: What The Move Says About Trump’s Second Term
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The DOJ charges former FBI director James Comey with making false statements and obstruction under pressure from President Trump. WSJ’s Annie Linskey explains what happens now.

#Comey #Trump #Shorts</article>

items.title: The Man Leading Christian Nationalism’s Rise Under Trump | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Pastor Doug Wilson is one of the most prominent advocates for Christian nationalism, the controversial movement to merge church and state. Wilson has spoken at the National Conservatism Conference and opened a branch of his church in Washington D.C., where one of his congregants is Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth. But many worry his fringe ideologies  are a threat to Democracy. 

WSJ’s Aaron Zitner sits down with him to discuss his growing influence under President Trump.

Chapters:
0:00 Wilson’s rise
1:06 Christian nationalism and its beliefs 
4:05 Threat to Democracy 
5:06 Proximity to power and Trump’s second term
6:25 Criticism 

#Trump #Religion #WSJ</article>

items.title: The Complex Tech Behind Growing Diamonds in Minutes | WSJ Tech Behind
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Diamonds grown in a lab aren’t just for jewelry; the fundamental technology is a rapidly advancing field with much broader applications. WSJ goes inside the global innovation center at Element Six, one of the world’s leading synthetic diamond labs, which grows diamonds for a wide and rapidly increasing range of uses — from drill bits to medical equipment and laser components. One day, its diamonds could even be used in semiconductors, but getting there will take some significant advances.

Chapters:
0:00 Synthetic diamonds
0:44 Synthesis
3:06 Diamond structure and applications 
4:28 How defects can be used

Tech Behind 
'The Tech Behind' explores the amazing engineering, computing, science and algorithms that power our favorite tech.

#Diamonds #Semiconductors #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Trump Wants to ‘Take Back’ Afghanistan’s Bagram Air Base
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>President Trump surprised the world when he said the U.S. was trying to regain some control of Bagram Air Base in Afghanistan. Here’s a look at the base’s strategic location.

#Trump #Afghanistan #Shorts</article>

items.title: Lockheed Martin Showed Us Their New F-35 Drone Tech | WSJ Equipped
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration’s DOGE initiative threatens the future of the F-35 program, which Elon Musk has labelled an obsolete boondoggle. But Lockheed Martin is using AI to evolve the jet, integrating drones into its system to boost capability and relevance. But after missing out on big contracts due to increased competition and tariffs, can Lockheed pivot to stay relevant?

WSJ visited Lockheed Martin’s facilities in Fort Worth, Texas, to get a closer look at the jets and their new capabilities. 

Chapters:
0:00 The F-35 with drones
0:50 Breaking down the F-35
2:16 Skunk Works: collaborative combat aircraft
4:48 Lockheed Martin’s difficulties 

Equipped
Equipped examines military innovation and tactics emerging around the world, breaking down the tech behind the weaponry and its potential impact.

#F35 #LockheedMartin #WSJ</article>

items.title: Charlie Kirk Full Memorial Service: Trump, Vance and Others Speak | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Mourners attend the memorial for slain conservative activist Charlie Kirk in Arizona. The service comes nearly two weeks after Kirk was fatally shot during an event on a Utah university campus. 

#CharlieKirk #Trump #WSJ</article>

items.title: WSJ's Chief Economics Correspondent on What's Next After Fed's Rate Cut
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Federal Reserve cut interest rates for the first time in nine months, with officials judging recent labor-market softness outweighed recent setbacks on inflation. WSJ’s Nick Timiraos explains what the central bank could do next. 

#Fed #JobMarket #Shorts</article>

items.title: Why Fed Rate Cuts Won't (Necessarily) Lead to Lower Mortgage Rates
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>High mortgage rates are keeping many Americans on the sidelines from buying or selling a home. WSJ’s Veronica Dagher explains why a potential interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve won’t lower mortgage rates in the short term.

#Fed #Mortgage #Shorts</article>

items.title: How Trump’s Strikes on Venezuelan Boats Could Spark Armed Conflict | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>President Trump has shown the U.S. can use lethal force to combat drug cartels in Venezuela and has repeatedly tied Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro to drug trafficking. The U.S. military carried out a strike against a drug-carrying boat from Venezuela killing eleven just days after the Pentagon deployed warships to the Caribbean to stop the flow of cocaine. 

WSJ’s Vera Bergengruen explains how the heightened rhetoric and military moves by the Trump administration and Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro could escalate.

Chapters:
0:00 U.S.-Venezuela tension
0:42 Trump’s crackdown on drug cartels
3:21 Pressure on Maduro

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Trump #Venezuela #WSJ</article>

items.title: Tracking ICE Arrests Under Trump’s Second Presidency
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>WSJ’s Brenna Smith explains how she verified social media videos of ICE arrests across New England, and what their locations reveal. 

#ICE #Trump #Shorts</article>

items.title: iOS 26: 26 New Features for Apple’s New iPhone Software Update | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Apple’s latest iPhone software iOS26 is the biggest visual makeover in years. New features include Liquid Glass, a built-in assistant to screen your calls, hold assist and more. AI features like live translation in calls and FaceTimes require Apple Intelligence, which only runs on iPhone 15 Pro and newer.

WSJ’s Joanna Stern shows you what’s new—and how to turn off the changes you don’t like.

Chapters:
0:00 iOS26
0:35 Lessen the glass
1:10 Glassify your home screen
1:34 Revert your phone app
1:53 Call screening
2:34 Hold assist 
3:18 Live translate calls
4:00 Filter unknown senders
4:32 Add messages backgrounds 
5:04 Add a poll to messages
5:22 Adjust snooze time 
5:36 Ask about screenshots 
6:00 Re-learn the camera app
6:24 Turn on adaptive power
6:44 More tips and tricks 

Tech Things With Joanna Stern
Everything is now a tech thing. In creative and humorous videos, WSJ senior personal tech columnist Joanna Stern explains and reviews the products, services and trends that are changing our world.

#Apple #iOS26 #WSJ</article>

items.title: Trump’s Tariffs Are Raking in Billions. Where Does It All Go? | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>President Trump repeatedly claims tariffs will reap “billions” of dollars for the U.S.—and so far, he’s right. According to the Penn Wharton Budget Model, the U.S. has collected more than $158 billion as of early September 2025. So where does that money go and how does Congress appropriate that money? 

WSJ examines how much money the U.S. has made from tariffs, how the money from tariffs is collected and where it ultimately goes. 

Chapters:
0:00 Tariff revenue 
0:36 How much the U.S. has made
2:16 How tariffs are collected
3:18 Where the money goes
4:38 Tariff legal challenges 

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Trump #Tariffs #WSJ</article>

items.title: Charlie Kirk Shooting: A Look at His Rise as a Conservative Activist
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>WSJ’s Aaron Zitner explains how conservative activist Charlie Kirk’s approach to persuading people made him an important figure in Republican politics, particularly in President Trump's orbit.

#CharlieKirk #Politics #Shorts</article>

items.title: How Taco Bell’s CEO Keeps Gen Z Obsessed | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Taco Bell is seeing sales rise despite an overall slump in the fast food industry. The restaurant chain recently made a big bet on chicken amid increased competition in the space: in the last year, McDonald’s, Chipotle and Wendy’s all launched chicken-focused campaigns. So how does Taco Bell keep retaining customers and staying ahead of trends in a crowded space?

WSJ spoke with CEO Sean Tresvant about how he keeps people coming back even as they pull 
back on spending.

Chapters:
0:00 Taco Bell sales
0:27 How Taco Bel is succeeding right now
0:57 Chicken investments
3:16 Staying on trends
4:28 Attracting Gen Z
6:10 Value offering
7:50 Growth 

#TacoBell #Food #WSJ</article>

items.title: I Wore a Suit That Made Me Feel Like I Was 80
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>It took two people to make WSJ’s Amy Dockser Marcus feel like a woman in her 80s.⁠
⁠
At the MIT AgeLab, which works on finding ways to improve life for the elderly, researchers let her spend a day in their age-simulation suit. She hoped the experience might bring greater insight into what it’s really like to age—and how to prepare.⁠

#MIT #Aging #Shorts</article>
